name: client
context: Build MCP clients that connect to servers, discover tools/resources/prompts, and route LLM tool calls
info: An MCP client is a component within a host application (like Claude Desktop, VS Code, or a custom chatbot) that maintains a connection to an MCP server. The client handles the initialization handshake, capability negotiation, and all protocol communication. It discovers available tools via `tools/list`, invokes them via `tools/call`, reads resources via `resources/read`, and retrieves prompts via `prompts/get`. The client also handles notifications for dynamic updates and manages transport connections (stdio subprocesses or HTTP). The Python SDK provides `ClientSession` with `stdio_client()` or `streamablehttp_client()` transports; the TypeScript SDK provides `Client` with `StdioClientTransport` or `StreamableHTTPClientTransport`.
triggers: discover tools from an MCP server
route tool calls to MCP servers
how to build an MCP client
connect my app to MCP servers

sections.title: Connecting to servers
sections.body: Use `stdio_client()` in Python or `StdioClientTransport` in TypeScript for local servers (launched as subprocesses). Use `streamablehttp_client()` or `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` for remote servers. Both return read/write streams wrapped in a `ClientSession` (Python) or `Client` (TypeScript). Always call `initialize()` after connecting to perform the capability negotiation handshake.

sections.title: Discovering capabilities
sections.body: After initialization, discover server capabilities: `list_tools()` returns available tools with names, descriptions, and input schemas. `list_resources()` returns available data sources. `list_prompts()` returns available prompt templates. All list operations support pagination via cursor. Register notification handlers for `list_changed` events to stay up-to-date when capabilities change dynamically.

sections.title: LLM tool routing
sections.body: The core integration pattern: 1) Aggregate tools from all connected MCP servers into a unified registry. 2) Pass tool definitions to the LLM alongside user messages. 3) When the LLM returns a tool_use block, route it to the correct MCP session based on tool name. 4) Call the tool via `call_tool()` and feed the result back to the LLM as a tool_result. This loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response.

sections.title: Managing multiple servers
sections.body: A host typically connects to multiple MCP servers simultaneously — each with its own ClientSession/Client instance. Aggregate tools across all sessions into a single list for the LLM. Maintain a mapping from tool name to session for routing. Handle server disconnections gracefully and re-aggregate tools when a server reconnects or sends a tools/list_changed notification.
